snapshot.name: Maria Alvarez
snapshot.teaser: 71-year-old female with idiopathic Parkinson disease and worsening gait speed, freezing episodes, and postural instability

history.chief_complaint: Shuffling gait and balance loss with Parkinson disease
history.hpi: 71-year-old female with 5-year history of Parkinson disease referred for worsening mobility over the past 4 months. Reports smaller steps, freezing in doorways, difficulty turning, and two near-falls when multitasking. Denies recent true falls but avoids community outings unless accompanied.

pain.location: Occasional low back and calf stiffness
pain.aggravating_factors: Initiation of gait, tight spaces, dual-tasking, fatigue, rising from low chair
pain.easing_factors: Medication on-time periods, external cueing, deliberate larger movements, rest

functional_goals: Walk through busy community spaces without freezing
Turn and back up more safely in the kitchen

gait.pattern: Hypokinetic gait with decreased step length, reduced arm swing, festination during turns
gait.observations: Freezing reproduced with doorway entry and pivot turns; delayed postural reactions; stooped posture

eval.notes_seed: Outpatient Parkinson disease case focused on amplitude training, turning, freezing, and fall prevention

subjective.chiefComplaint: Walking is slower and I freeze when I turn or go through doors
subjective.historyOfPresentIllness: Symptoms have worsened gradually over 4 months despite medication adherence. Patient notices best mobility about 45-60 minutes after medication and greater freezing when rushed or distracted.
subjective.painPattern: Fluctuates with medication timing and fatigue
subjective.aggravatingFactors: Turning, backing up, doorways, dual-tasking, crowded places, getting up from soft couch
subjective.easingFactors: Counting out loud, visual floor targets, large purposeful steps, rest breaks
subjective.functionalLimitations: Slow community ambulation, freezing with turns, cautious shower transfers, difficulty carrying objects while walking
subjective.priorLevel: Independent community ambulator without freezing episodes; attended church and family outings regularly
subjective.patientGoals: Move bigger, walk faster, and stop freezing when turning
subjective.redFlags: No recent syncope, chest pain, or acute neurologic change
subjective.additionalHistory: Lives with spouse in single-story home; uses phone reminders for medication schedule

objective.text: Pleasant, oriented patient evaluated during medication 'on' period. Mild hypomimia and soft voice noted.

inspection.visual: Stooped posture, reduced trunk rotation, diminished spontaneous arm swing bilaterally left greater than right

palpation.findings: Generalized rigidity through trunk and hips; no focal tenderness

neuro.screening: Bradykinesia with finger tapping and alternating movements, mild rigidity, impaired anticipatory and reactive postural control, freezing triggered by turning and narrow spaces

functional.assessment: MiniBESTest 17/28; TUG 15.8 sec, worsens to 19.6 sec with cognitive dual task; 10MWT self-selected 0.74 m/s. Sit-to-stand requires 2 attempts from low chair without hands.

assessment.primaryImpairments: Bradykinesia, gait hypokinesia, freezing with turning/doorways, impaired postural control, decreased amplitude of movement, and reduced dual-task mobility associated with Parkinson disease.
assessment.bodyFunctions: Impaired movement scaling, reduced anticipatory balance strategies, axial rigidity, diminished trunk rotation, and slowed transfer mechanics.
assessment.activityLimitations: Turning, backing up, narrow-space negotiation, community ambulation, sit-to-stand from low surfaces, and carrying items while walking.
assessment.participationRestrictions: Avoids busy community events and family outings due to fear of freezing and near-falls.
assessment.ptDiagnosis: G20.A1 - Parkinson's disease without dyskinesia, without mention of fluctuations; R26.81 - Unsteadiness on feet.
assessment.prognosticFactors: Strong insight and family support; responds to external cueing; chronic progressive disease limits full restoration but good potential for improved mobility and fall reduction with skilled PT.
assessment.clinicalReasoning: Presentation is consistent with hypokinetic gait and balance deficits due to Parkinson disease. Skilled PT is indicated for amplitude-based training, cueing strategies, transfer practice, turning/freezing interventions, and progressive balance challenge to maintain safe community participation.

plan.treatmentPlan: Amplitude-based movement training, gait cueing, turning and doorway practice, reactive and anticipatory balance, sit-to-stand progression, dual-task mobility, and caregiver education for freezing strategies.
plan.patientEducation: Medication timing relative to exercise, freezing management with cueing, home fall prevention, big-movement strategy practice, and energy conservation during community activity.

goal_1.goalText: Improve self-selected gait speed from 0.74 m/s to at least 0.9 m/s within 6-8 weeks.

goal_2.goalText: Reduce TUG from 15.8 sec to 12 sec or less within 8 weeks.

goal_3.goalText: Demonstrate safe 180-degree turns without freezing in 4 out of 5 trials using internal or external cueing strategies within 6 weeks.

goal_4.goalText: Improve MiniBESTest from 17/28 to at least 21/28 within 8 weeks.

ex_1.exerciseText: Daily amplitude warm-up: exaggerated sit-to-stand, reaches, stepping, and trunk rotation - 10 minutes, daily.

ex_2.exerciseText: Overground gait with visual and auditory cueing focused on bigger steps and arm swing - 10-15 minutes, daily.

ex_3.exerciseText: Doorway and turning practice using stop-reset-big step strategy - 10 reps each, daily.

ex_4.exerciseText: Step and reach balance sequence with multidirectional stepping - 3x8 each direction, 5x/week.

diagnosisCodes.description: Parkinson's disease without dyskinesia, without mention of fluctuations
diagnosisCodes.label: G20.A1 - Parkinson's disease without dyskinesia, without mention of fluctuations

ordersReferrals.details: Coordinate with speech therapy if voice or cognitive-communication decline becomes more limiting.
Recommend community exercise program with Parkinson-specific group class after skilled episode of care.
